chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Criminal Bail Application No.1116 of 2025 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATUREs OF JUDGEs For hearing of bail application ------------------------------------------ 22.05.2025 Mr. Sohrab Ahmed Meo, advocate for applicant Ms. Seema Zaidi, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh Complainant present IO/ASI Ghulam Yasin Bhayo of Gulistan-e-Johar, Karachi ------------------------------------ Applicant/accused Muhammad Ameen son of Muhammad Aslam seeks post arrest bail in FIR No.189/2025, registered at P.S. Gulistan-e-Johar, Karachi for offence under section 397, 448, 511, 34, PPC, after rejection of his bail plea by learned Additional Sessions Judge-XII, Karachi East vide order dated 16.04.2025. 2. Facts involved in the instant case are that on 18.03.2025 at 09:00 a.m. sister of complainant informed him that some persons, male and female, after cutting safety wires trying to enter the house, she asked him to reach early and report the matter to police, when he reached the house of his sister, he saw that one Alto car was standing there and some persons duly armed with weapons were sitting in it. He knocked the door, his sister was yelling because she was made hostage by accused persons. Due to hue and cry, mohalla people gathered, in the meanwhile police also reached, they jumped over the wall. Present applicant and 2/3 females were available in house, who made his sister hostage. They were apprehended, present applicant disclosed his name as Muhammad Ameen whereas females succeeded to run away by taking advantage of rush. Hence the subject FIR. 3. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in this case; that there is no criminal record of applicant; that he has no nexus with co-accused Abdul Lateef Chishti, who is a hardened and desperate criminal, involved in so many criminal cases and there is sufficient material available on record which requires further inquiry in terms of section 497, Cr.PC. 3.

Hence the subject FIR. 3. Learned counsel for applicant submits that applicant is innocent and he has been falsely implicated in this case; that there is no criminal record of applicant; that he has no nexus with co-accused Abdul Lateef Chishti, who is a hardened and desperate criminal, involved in so many criminal cases and there is sufficient material available on record which requires further inquiry in terms of section 497, Cr.PC. 3. On the other hand, learned Additional Prosecutor General Sindh, assisted by complainant, opposed for grant of bail on the ground that applicant was apprehended at spot by complainant party with the help of people of vicinity, who gathered at the spot and injured him. She further submits that applicant belongs to the gang of Abdul Lateef Chishti group, who is a Qabza Mafia. 4. Heard learned counsel for applicant, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh, complainant in person and perused the material available on record. 5. From tentative assessment of material available on record it appears that applicant was apprehended red handed at the spot by the complainant with the help of mohalla people, who got him injured, he was produced before learned Magistrate, such fact is confirmed by perusal of record. IO present in Court submits that applicant belongs to a hardened and desperate criminal gang, which is involved in Qabza, robbery and other criminal cases. Since the applicant was arrested at the spot, which connects him with the commission of alleged offence. No case for further inquiry in the guilt of applicant is made out. Therefore, instant criminal bail application for post arrest bail is dismissed. 6. Needless to mention here that observations made herein above are tentative in nature and would not prejudice the case of either party at trial. J U D G E Gulsher/PS
